scenario_results.reason: Returned an L7 delivery architecture recommendation with assumptions, TLS and WAF posture, failure modes, observability, rollback, and source/evidence traceability.
Compared DNS steering, global load balancing, regional load balancing, and L4 passthrough by protocol, state, latency, failover, DNS, route, and ownership constraints.
Produced operator-safe health-check, drain, failover, retry, backoff, rollback, and stakeholder communication runbook steps.
Evaluated algorithm, stickiness, keepalive, connection reuse, autoscaling, saturation, idempotency, and monitoring tradeoffs under burst load.
Isolated DNS, resolver, TTL, route, firewall, and target-health evidence before recommending staged mitigation and rollback.
Handled TLS certificate rotation failures with chain, SNI, cipher, protocol, termination-point, validation, and authority-boundary checks.
Addressed zone impairment and asymmetric routing with stateful-inspection evidence, partial-health caveats, drain choices, and capacity-preserving mitigation.
Flagged WAF and timeout policy regressions and produced sequenced implementation notes with validation and rollback gates.
Produced a service disposition that separated confirmed mitigation facts from missing logs, synthetic checks, and incident evidence.
Rejected endpoint lifecycle ownership and routed to endpoint specialists while retaining only network ingress evidence questions.
Rejected identity authentication and access policy design and routed to identity specialists.
Rejected application code and pure application performance work while defining conditions for a separate network-path review.
Refused procurement, policy override, and fail-open workaround authority and created an escalation packet.
Declared low confidence due to undocumented preview behavior and missing target-health evidence, then returned to orchestrator.
Separated the load-balancing portion from Kubernetes application, DNS, IAM, and firewall ownership and returned cross-functional work to the orchestrator.
Escalated repeated 502 failures with prior actions, conflicting records, unresolved hypotheses, owner map, and vendor-support context.
Escalated when safety-critical tenant context, authority, rollback ownership, and policy precedence were missing instead of treating cross-industry context as cosmetic.
Adapted to tenant ALB, Route 53, WAF, certificate, monitoring, change, and ADR records while preserving public baseline traceability and specification-based materialization constraints.

notes: Scenario coverage exceeds the legacy 10-scenario minimum and preserves the legacy design, troubleshooting, tradeoff, edge-case, refusal, and low-confidence rigor.
The suite covers every issue-required output shape, refusal trigger, escalation trigger, service/support workflow family, and cross-industry domain scenario family.
All required shared, service-support-and-resolution, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to passing scenarios in evaluation/functionality-map.json.
Functionality coverage is complete and runtime remains specification-based with no trusted built implementation claim.
